narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A user profile service where compute is correctly sized and operating in healthy ranges, but the database is significantly over-provisioned and operating at very low utilization. The right action is downsize the database only — compute and read replicas should not change.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests mixed-state handling. The agent must recommend action on one tier and explicit no-action on another in the same scenario. Failure mode caught: agent recommends across-the-board rightsizing when only one tier needs it.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Downsize the database from db.r6g.2xlarge to db.r6g.large. Leave compute and read replicas unchanged. Expected savings: ~$1,400/month.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation identifies the DB as over-provisioned. A great recommendation explicitly notes that compute is correctly sized and must not be touched — the mixed nature of the scenario is what makes the discriminating judgment matter.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: Compute metrics stayed in healthy ranges (unchanged), database db_query_p95_latency_ms stayed under 100ms, SLA 99.9% preserved, monthly cost dropped by approximately $1,400.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across user-profile-service cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Downsize database from db.r6g.2xlarge to db.r6g.large. Leave compute tier and read replicas unchanged.
target_recommendation.expected_performance_impact: No regression on compute (unchanged); database stays under 100ms p95 latency
